Sports.title: Tom Aspinall issues fight update after double eye surgery
Sports.content:
Tom Aspinall issues fight update after double eye surgery

Tom Aspinall has not fought since his ill-fated UFC heavyweight title defence against Ciryl Gane last October but believes is inching closer to a return

View Image Tom Aspinall speaks out

Tom Aspinall hopes to return to the cage this year following his double eye surgery.

‌



The UFC heavyweight champion has not fought since his ill-fated title defence against Ciryl Gane last October. Aspinall was poked in the eyes by the Frenchman and the fight called off and deemed a no-contest. Aspinall has since undergone surgery on both eyes but has resumed training and hopes to fight before the end of 2026.

‌



“I hope so, I hope so. I think so," he said when asked on The Ariel Helwani Show if he believes he will fight this year. “They’re giving me the indication that everything’s going in the right direction and improving fast. There was a time when things were all up in the air, because we had some complications with it, but everything now is moving good, and I’m feeling a lot better.

‌



FOLLOW OUR FIGHTING FB PAGE! Latest boxing and MMA news, analysis and much more via our dedicated Facebook page

Jon Jones completely destroys Dana White over UFC White House fight claim READ MORE :

Dana White shuts down Jon Jones' claim over UFC White House card READ MORE :

“And I can really see the difference in the way the vision is, because vision is something that you use obviously – every second of every day. I’m feeling really good about it at the moment.”

‌



When Aspinall does return, he will be slated to face the winner of Gane's interim title clash with Alex Pereira which has been booked as the co-main event on the UFC's White House card. And he said of the booking: “I mean, who else is going to fight? The division’s on its ass. There’s not many big fights to make, is there? So they’ve got to do what they’ve got to do. Obviously, I want to fight the winner of that.

"I'm just waiting now to get the green light, the all clear from the doctor to start training properly again. As soon as we can start training properly again, full contact in the gym, we will be getting a fight date booked to fight the winner. I'm just waiting for the clearance to be medically cleared, and then we go. As soon as the doctor clears me, we’ll let the UFC know and we’ll be looking to book a fight soon as possible."

Aspinall also leapt to the defence of Jon Jones who has demanded he be released from the UFC after negotiations broke down over his appearance on the White House card. “This is going to probably be crazy to a lot of people listening, but why is the UFC not giving Jon the respect he deserves financially? That's crazy to me," he added.

Article continues below

“If anyone should be getting paid a lot in this sport, it should be Jon Jones for the stuff that he's done. That's me saying that after the history that I've had with Jon Jones. If anybody should be getting money in this sport, it's Jon Jones. They offered him $30 million to fight me. So why have they not offered it again? I don't understand. These are the guys who brought in the revenue for over 10 years. They need to be getting paid what they deserve, in my opinion."

Sports.title: Jockey hit with suspension over incident in horse race as stewards issue statement
Sports.content:
Jockey hit with suspension over incident in horse race as stewards issue statement

6ft 4in horse racing jockey Jack Andrews has been suspended after an investigation

View Image Jack Andrews has been handed a ban.

Jockey Jack Andrews, known as the world's tallest jockey at six feet four inches, has been handed a 12-day suspension after taking the wrong course and missing a fence at Stratford-on-Avon yesterday. Andrews was leading the race on Xcitations in a 2m1f handicap chase when he mistakenly skipped the water jump at the fifth fence.

‌



It had been pre-agreed that the sixth fence would be left out for chase races. However, aboard the 11- year-old horse trained by Pam Sly, Andrews became confused.

‌



He quickly pulled up his mount upon realising his error, but this resulted in a suspension.

‌



Cheltenham races LIVE: Best tips for every race from day one of the 2026 festival READ MORE :

Who is the favourite to win the Champion Hurdle? READ MORE :

The stewards' inquiry stated: "An inquiry was held to consider whether Xcitations, ridden by Jack Andrews, had appeared to take the incorrect course after jumping the first fence in the home straight on the first circuit.

"The rider was interviewed and shown recordings of the incident. Andrews was suspended for 12 days for taking the wrong course as he had failed to jump the water jump and subsequently pulled the gelding up."

The race was eventually won by Harel Du Marais, ridden by James Bowen, by a margin of three and a quarter lengths.

‌



Speaking to Racing TV, Bowen said: "He switched off well, which he hasn't done so well previously, and I was able to be a lot more handy on him than usual. I ended up challenging for the lead too soon but once I got by I thought I was always going to stay there."

Andrews is recognised as the tallest jockey in the world because he stands over a foot taller than his typical counterparts. He has still reached the summit of jump racing, but has to follow a rigorous diet to maintain his weight.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



He said in 2023: "I don't know of anyone riding under rules who is taller than me. But I'm very self-critical and constantly trying to make myself better, especially with my riding.

"The biggest compliment anyone can give me is that I don't look big on a horse. But for my diet, I have to have a daily routine to keep my weight down. Gaining weight is an issue but I keep it under control.

"I'll have a coffee on my way to work then a yogurt, fruit and some granola for breakfast. Lunch is a yogurt bar and a banana, whilst dinner is some protein like chicken and another yogurt - I eat every meal, just not a lot.

Article continues below

"That being said, when I have a cheat day and go out I eat properly! I don't pick salads, that's for sure. I go all out."

Sports.title: Who is Megan Nicholls? ITV Racing star's famous dad and ex with Tottenham connections
Sports.content:
Who is Megan Nicholls? ITV Racing star's famous dad and ex with Tottenham connections

ITV Racing presenter Megan Nicholls is the daughter of a horse racing legend and retired from her own career in the saddle

View 3 Images Megan Nicholls' has established her own racing legacy beyond her famous dad (Image: Instagram/@meg_nicholls97)

Megan Nicholls will once again lend her expertise to ITV Racing at the Cheltenham Festival this week. The 27-year-old is the offspring of one of the most prolific trainers of the modern era, Paul Nicholls.

‌



The excitement from Prestbury Park is set to kick off as bettors gather for the pinnacle of the National Hunt season. Over 200,000 spectators attended the four-day Festival last year, and similar numbers are expected to turn up in 2026.

‌



For those not making the trip to the Cotswolds, ITV will deliver its customary coverage throughout the meeting. And former Flat jockey Nicholls will be one of the broadcaster's standout personalities.

‌



Nicholls hung up her riding boots at the tender age of 25 to concentrate on media and has since become a regular feature on ITV. Here, Mirror Sport delves into her illustrious racing family, as well as her past and present love interests.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Nicholls' father, Paul, is a legendary figure in the National Hunt arena. He has been crowned Champion Trainer an 14 occasions and operates from Ditcheat in Somerset.

‌



Cheltenham Festival-winning trainer Evan Williams found guilty of attacking dog walker READ MORE :

Nicholls Sr has trained more than 3,500 winners and clinched the Cheltenham Gold Cup four times. He has also trained some of the sport's most celebrated horses, including Kauto Star, Denman and Big Buck's.

Nicholls followed in her father's footsteps by becoming a jockey, but she competed on the Flat, notching up 110 victories over the course of her career. Her sister, Olive, is an amateur National Hunt rider.

View 3 Images The former jockey has thrived as an ITV presenter, with Ed Chamberlain as her mentor (Image: Instagram/@meg_nicholls97)

‌



In 2022, at the age of 25, Nicholls chose to step away from her riding career to dedicate herself fully to bloodstock and media. She made her ITV Racing debut in 2021 and hasn't looked back since.

Explaining her decision, she previously told the Racing Post: "I felt my riding career had hit a point where I was going back down. Unfortunately, not everyone is blessed with natural talent and you can work as hard as you want, but when you have people ahead of you it's hard."

FREE William Hill Shop Bets inside your Mirror every day of Cheltenham Don't miss a FREE shop bet inside your favourite racing paper every day of the Cheltenham festival! Plus, get expert inside from our top tipsters with pullouts every day from Tuesday March 10 - Friday March 13, 2026 covering the runners, riders, and everything you need to make the 102nd Cheltenham festival the biggest ever. Find out more here. Bet 18+ only. Terms and conditions apply. See paper for details.

‌



Since her debut, Nicholls has regularly appeared alongside the likes of Ed Chamberlin, Mick Fitzgerald and AP McCoy for ITV. She also presents on Racing TV and is expanding her remit.

While she left riding behind, Nicholls maintained a high-profile relationship following her retirement. She was once romantically linked with Group 1-winning jockey Kevin Stott.

The couple met while riding out in Dubai and started dating around early 2020. Stott had previously undergone a trial with Premier League club Tottenham but returned to riding after his football dream didn't materialise.

‌



View 3 Images It's understood Nicholls is now linked with jockey prospect Tristan Durrell (Image: Instagram/@meg_nicholls97)

Nicholls and Stott were together for two years before splitting up. Now, judging by social media posts, she appears to be romantically linked with up-and-coming jockey Tristan Durrell.

Durrell secured one of the biggest victories of his fledgling career earlier this season when he guided the Dan Skelton-trained Panic Attack to Coral Gold Cup success. Images of Durrell and Nicholls enjoying dinner dates regularly feature on the ITV star's Instagram.

Sports.title: BBC suffers another major event loss in brutal hit to sporting coverage
Sports.content:
BBC suffers another major event loss in brutal hit to sporting coverage

The BBC's sporting coverage has been damaged yet again with the loss of a historic event

View 2 Images The Boat Race will no longer be broadcast on the BBC (Image: PA)

The BBC has suffered another setback ahead of this year's Boat Race, after it emerged the broadcaster will also lose its radio coverage of the historic sporting event. Channel 4's five-year agreement to replace the BBC as the television broadcaster of the Boat Race was confirmed last year, dealing a significant blow to its sporting portfolio.

‌



In a further setback, the BBC has now been replaced as the race's radio partner by Times Radio, which has secured a two-year agreement. Live commentary of both the men's and women's Oxford v Cambridge races will also be available on the platform.

‌



The Boat Race, which was first held in 1829 and has been held annually since 1856, marks the first sports rights agreement for Times Radio. The BBC had been the long-standing home of the Boat Race, with its first broadcast coverage in 1924, and had remained so, apart from ITV holding TV rights from 2005-09 and LBC holding the radio rights from 2005-10.

‌



Tim Levell, the programme director of Times Radio, said of the new collaboration: "We're proud to partner with the Boat Race in a three-year deal through to 2028. The Boat Race is a brilliant fit for Times Radio and our listeners, for whom this is a much-loved annual tradition.

BBC won't air Boat Race for first time in nearly a century due to controversial approach READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



"We know they'll look forward to tuning in on race day with all the buzz and atmosphere from the Thames and exclusive radio commentary of the races themselves. We're especially delighted to support the Women's Race as it approaches its centenary in 2027."

The loss of the Boat Race only heightens reported concerns among BBC staff about the broadcaster's future, as reported by the Daily Mail, amid a steadily shrinking portfolio of sporting events . The broadcaster has lost rights to several sports events in 2026, including England men's home and away matches for the Six Nations.

View 2 Images The BBC has already lost exclusive rights to Wimbledon coverage (Image: Julian Finney, Getty Images)

Article continues below

In addition to the Six Nations, the BBC also lost the rights to broadcast the Commonwealth Games this year. It marks the first time since 1954 that the BBC won't be the main broadcaster for this summer's Commonwealth Games.

Staff are now reportedly worried about the future of Wimbledon. While the BBC still holds the live rights for the Grand Slam until 2027, they've lost the exclusive rights to the tournament's highlights.

Sports.title: 'I'm an ITV racing expert – I have seven selections for Cheltenham today including 11-1 shot'
Sports.content:
'I'm an ITV racing expert – I have seven selections for Cheltenham today including 11-1 shot'

ITV Racing's Richard Hoiles has given his predictions for seven races at Cheltenham

View Image Matt Chapman offered his seven selections for Cheltenham today (Image: AFP/Getty Images)

The Cheltenham Festival is back with its 102nd edition in full swing. The Greatest Show on Turf is poised to deliver once again, with hundreds of thousands of attendees expected to enjoy the action.

‌



As we enter day one of the festival, ITV commentator Richard Hoiles is already prepared with his seven selections today, including a 10-1 shout with the tipster getting the show off the road in a big way through his Betway blog.

‌



13:20: Supreme Novices' Hurdle (Grade 1)

This includes one of the most hyped horses of the week, Mighty Park, who comes to the Supreme rather than the Turners, with his trainer making lofty comparisons to past stable stars. He locks horns with the major British hope Old Park Star from the yard that has given us Altior, Shishkin and Constitution Hill.

‌



He has been a little more visible than Mighty Park, having moved from Paul Nicholls at the end of last season, but at the prevailing prices TALK THE TALK is preferred. His form lines with the likes of Skylight Hustle, Ballyfad and El Rasko Grey will be well tested this week, and it was his defeat of the latter pair at the Dublin Racing Festival that caught the eye. Poorly placed in a steadily run race, he produced a very taking turn of foot to lead on the line — a performance which deserves upgrading — and his claims look to have more substance than some of his rivals at this stage.

14:00: Singer Arkle Challenge Trophy Novices’ Chase (Grade 1)

The Arkle looks to be an Anglo‑Irish clash between LULAMBA for the home team, up against Irish pair Kopek Des Bordes and Kargese, both of whom won at last year’s Festival. Kopek Des Bordes won the Supreme and at that stage looked a real potential star, but suffered defeat afterwards at Punchestown and comes here off the back of just one run over fences in November.

Connections opted to miss the Dublin Racing Festival due to the deep ground, and the connections of Kargese may wish they had done the same, as she seemed to have a very gruelling race when narrowly beaten by Romeo Coolio (who goes for the Brown Advisory). They walked home that day, and how she fares could be an early indicator as to whether those who ran at Leopardstown may find that races there have left a mark.

‌



Lulamba has the five‑year‑old stat (0–12 since the allowances were changed) to overcome, but has followed a well‑trodden path for Henderson horses, including a run outside novice company when winning the Game Spirit at Newbury. By his trainer’s own admission he is likely to prove more of a Ryanair/King George horse down the line, so the first four fences will be crucial, but his strength in the finish up the hill can prove decisive.

Thousands of Brits descend on Benidorm for cheaper Cheltenham alternative READ MORE :

Cheltenham Festival-winning trainer Evan Williams found guilty of attacking dog walker READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



14:40: McCoy Contractors Juvenile Handicap Hurdle (each-way)

The first handicap of the week is the repositioned Fred Winter, where only 10 lb cover the 22 runners, and one of the leading fancies, Munsif, finds himself as first reserve, so he will need something to come out before 13:00 on Friday to get a run. This is a bit of a minefield, but it could be the first of a couple of birthday wins for the Green and Gold courtesy of MANLAGA.

He first caught my attention when he appeared at the Newbury gallops morning in November, accompanying some higher‑profile stablemates, including Constitution Hill. A winner in France, he made his ground quite quickly when beating the re‑opposing Pourquoi Pas Papa at Haydock, with The Mighty Celt in third, and he can confirm the form here.

15:20: The Trustmarque Ultima Handicap Chase

The McManus silks can also be to the fore in the first handicap chase of the week, the Ultima. The surprising decision to bypass Kelso with Grand National favourite Iroko and run here instead sets a bit of a poser. Up until that point, JAGWAR had been a solid favourite, so why is a backup deemed necessary?

‌



It may well be that connections simply want two strings to their bow as part of the birthday battalions, and Jagwar still has plenty going for him. Rapidly progressive last season and winner of the Plate at the Festival, it was felt earlier in the campaign that he could be running in Graded races by now.

Instead, he has twice placed in handicaps: the first time undone by a steady pace, and latterly, somewhat surprisingly, not getting past Donnacha despite travelling strongly. He steps up in trip for the first time (a top‑four finish would qualify him for the Grand National, for which he holds an entry) and that can bring further improvement. Traditionally this is a race for the home team, but the Irish‑trained The Short Go deserves a mention, having finished fifth last year despite a bad error halfway. Repeat performers tend to do well in this.

16:00:

The feature race on Day 1 is the Champion Hurdle, a division which has endured a chaotic 12 months since this day last year, when the falls of Constitution Hill and State Man presented the race to Golden Ace. She is the only one of the trio to make it back this year and is joined, as last year, by Brighterdaysahead (who ran poorly last season), but this time also by Lossiemouth, who arrives with cheekpieces applied for the first time.

‌



Her record at the track and faster ground can see her competitive, but THE NEW LION can give away the 7 lb to the mares. He laid the infamous Challow bogey to rest when winning the Turners at last year’s Festival and won the International here last time when ridden for a turn of foot, in a race marred by the injury to another absentee today, Sir Gino. Dan Skelton has only ever had two big‑priced runners in the race before but is represented in all four feature races this week, and success here would virtually seal his first trainers’ title. Last season the head‑to‑head clashes with Willie Mullins all went Closutton’s way, but the tables can be turned..

16:40: Sun Racing Plate Handicap Chase (each-way)

The last two races on the opening day can again see Skelton on the mark with MADARA, for whom the reapplication of cheekpieces could be a big help. Off for over a year before running at Wetherby in December, he looked to be given a prep for this last time at Kempton. Placed off this mark here in the past, he looks to hold a leading chance.

17:20: National Hunt Challenge Cup Novices' Handicap Chase

Finally, in the National Hunt Chase, ONE BIG BANG 11/1 (EW) can gain a reward for his consistency. Fifth in the Pertemps Final over hurdles last year, he needs producing late in his races as he can think the job is done, but the test of stamina looks right up his street. The slight concern about his desire to win makes him more of an each‑way angle.

Article continues below

Sports.title: Man City vs Liverpool set to be denied Sunday 4pm kick-off time for FA Cup showdown
Sports.content:
Man City vs Liverpool set to be denied Sunday 4pm kick-off time for FA Cup showdown

Man City take on rivals Liverpool in the quarter-final of the competition with both sides just one win away from Wembley.

View 2 Images The FA Cup is into the quarter-final stage. (Image: Andrew Kearns - CameraSport via Getty Images)

Manchester City and Liverpool’s FA Cup quarter-final clash is not expected to take place in the normal showpiece slot of 4pm on Sunday, due to the Champions League. The clash between the two Premier League giants is the standout tie as the last eight teams battle to make it to Wembley.

‌



However, both teams’ possible participation in the Champions League could restrict when the match can be played. The quarter-final is scheduled for the weekend of April 4, with the date and time of the fixture yet to be confirmed.

‌



However, the Champions League quarter-finals will take place just days later. Should either Man City or Liverpool get through their round of 16 clashes, then it will have an impact.

‌



Brentford boss slams 'disgusting' comments after panenka penalty miss READ MORE :

Paul Scholes reveals Michael Carrick reaction after backlash to controversial Man Utd post READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

City travel to take on Real Madrid in their first leg on Wednesday night, before the return leg next Tuesday (17 March). Liverpool, meanwhile, travel to Turkey to take on Galatasaray tonight (5.45pm kick-off), with the second leg taking place next Wednesday (18 March) at Anfield.

In the last FA Cup round, TV slots ranged from Friday night, three games on a Saturday, two games on the Sunday and one on Monday night. If they remain similar, then it’s likely City vs Liverpool will be played in one of the slots on either Friday or Saturday.

‌



City booked their place in the quarter-final of the competition thanks to a 3-1 away win over Newcastle on Saturday night. 24 hours earlier, Liverpool were the first team to claim their place in the final eight thanks to a 3-1 away win over Wolves.

View 2 Images Liverpool beat Wolves 3-1 in their FA Cup fifth-round clash. (Image: Liverpool FC/Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

The other quarter-finals will see Chelsea vs League One side Port Vale, Championship Southampton hosting Arsenal, and the all-Premier League tie between West Ham and Leeds.

‌



The scheduling problem could also impact both Chelsea and Arsenal, should they make it through their own Champions League knockout games.

Chelsea face PSG away on Wednesday night, while Arsenal are also away against Bayer Leverkusen, with the return legs taking place next Tuesday.

Should all four English teams make it through their ties, then it is likely to leave broadcasters with no choice as to put West Ham vs Leeds United in the Sunday 4pm slot.
